Willardmechem: ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Result
  I found prior local update notes: the last documented Emily upgrade was 2026.4.2 -> 2026.4.5 on April 6, 2026, plus a local openclaw-update-guard script
  meant to verify model/auth state after upgrades.

  OpenClaw on Emily is now updated to stable 2026.5.12 from 2026.5.4 using the official openclaw update path. The gateway is healthy again: openclaw gateway
  status --json shows service active/running, RPC ok, and server version 2026.5.12. Telegram also came back cleanly in the post-restart logs: provider start,
  menu rebuild, and isolated polling ingress all started without new errors.

  What Changed
  The official updater changed .openclaw/openclaw.json and .config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service. The config migrated the default model wiring from
  openai-codex/gpt-5.4 to openai/gpt-5.4 with the bundled codex plugin/runtime, while preserving:

  - gateway.trustedProxies = ["127.0.0.1"]
  - gateway.nodes.allowCommands = []
  - channels.telegram.enabled = true
  - agents.defaults.workspace = /home/willard/.openclaw/workspace

  The updater also rewrote auth-profiles.json via the existing sync flow; it is still present and openclaw models status --json reports the OAuth profile as
  ok.

  What Was Backed Up
  I created a timestamped backup set in /home/willard/openclaw-update-backups/20260516-122554 containing:

  - openclaw.json
  - openclaw-gateway.service
  - sync-codex-auth-to-openclaw
  - gateway.systemd.env
  - auth-profiles.json
  - pre-update workspace manifest
  - pre-update openclawtom manifest
  - pre-update critical-file stat baseline

  The official updater also created its own .bak copies under ~/.openclaw/.

  Manual Fixes
  One manual fix was required. The official updater removed the gateway pre-start auth sync hook from .config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service, which
  violated your “do not remove or overwrite the Codex auth sync setup” rule. I restored:

  - ExecStartPre=/home/willard/.openclaw/bin/sync-codex-auth-to-openclaw

  After that, I reloaded systemd and restarted the gateway. The current service status shows the ExecStartPre hook ran successfully.

  Verification

  - Gateway healthy: yes
  - Telegram healthy: yes, based on clean post-restart startup logs
  - Codex auth sync setup still exists: yes, after restoring the dropped ExecStartPre; sync path/service units also still exist
  - Workspace continuity intact: yes, full manifest diff was clean
  - Tom files untouched: yes, full manifest diff was clean
  - Security audit not worse: no

  Remaining Risks / Follow-up
  The only remaining regression is security-audit noise, not an exposure change. Before the update, openclaw security audit --deep was 0 warn / 2 info. After
  the update, openclaw security audit is 1 warn / 2 info because the new bundled codex plugin is recorded with an unpinned npm install spec in ~/.openclaw/
  plugins/installs.json (@openclaw/codex instead of a pinned spec). I left that alone because fixing it conservatively would require extra plugin-registry
  churn beyond the core upgrade.

  If you want, I can do a separate narrow pass just for that audit warning.

Willardmechem: • Root Cause

  The Mac node was not reading a stale token from ~/.openclaw/service-env. That env file has no OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN.

  The mismatch came from ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json: gateway.mode was still "local". In that mode, openclaw node run --host emily... uses the
  Mac’s local gateway.auth.token (1e9a...), not gateway.remote.token (a2c...). Emily rejected that as AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH.

  Fix Applied

  Changed:

  "gateway": {
    ...
    "mode": "remote"
  }

  in /Users/willardmechem/.openclaw/openclaw.json, then restarted the Mac node LaunchAgent:

  openclaw node restart

  The browser profile config was preserved: defaultProfile = billchrome, browser enabled, and node browser proxy still allows billchrome.

  Verification

  Mac side:

  - ai.openclaw.node is running with pid 8018.
  - node.err.log has not changed since May 16 16:27:34 2026; no new AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH or protocol mismatch after the fix.
  - node.log now has a fresh Browser control service ready (profiles=3) entry.

  Emily side:

  - Gateway journal since 2026-05-16 16:28:00 shows no token_mismatch, AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH, unauthorized, or protocol mismatch.
  - Emily gateway has an established connection on 127.0.0.1:18789 through the Tailscale/Serve proxy.
  - Running Emily’s current OpenClaw binary reports the Mac browser path again:

  billchrome: stopped [default] [existing-session]
  profile: billchrome
  enabled: true
  running: true
  userDataDir: /Users/willardmechem/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome
  detectedBrowser: chrome

  So the Mac node/browser host is connected and browser-capable again via billchrome.

  Biggest Tradeoffs / Risks

  Shipping dominates many larger organization products; several useful cabinet and dish-rack items become unattractive once shipping is
  included.

  The cleanest Apexstijl fit is not always the highest-margin item. Some cheap tools have strong margins but feel generic or gimmicky.

  Adhesive, suction, magnet, and pump products need sample testing before import because failure risk is mostly functional, not visual.

  Cutting boards fit the new kitchen direction, but claims like “medical-grade,” “antibacterial,” and “titanium” should be softened unless
  verified.

  1. 304 Stainless Steel Cutting Board.
  2. Roll-Up Over-Sink Drying Rack.
  3. Stainless Steel Paper Towel Rack.
  4. Sink-Mount Soap Dispenser Pump.
  5. Over-Sink Telescopic Drainer Tray.

  Important distinction:

  - Base currency: CZK. This is what product prices are stored in.
  - Payout currency: EUR bank payout account. This does not make product prices EUR.

  Markets are active:

  Root Cause
  The current prices were almost certainly entered manually as USD-style retail numbers into a CZK-base Shopify store.

• Factual Findings

  1. Store base currency is CZK. Shopify admin/API reports currency: CZK, money format Kč, and General settings show Czech Koruna (CZK Kč)
     under Currency display.
  2. Payout currency is different. Shopify Payments is active, and the payout account shown earlier is an EUR bank account. That does not
     make the store EUR-based. Shopify distinguishes store currency from payout currency: store currency is used for product pricing/admin
     reports; payout currency is what Shopify Payments deposits to the bank.
  3. Product prices are stored as CZK values. Current product prices like 19.95, 24.95, 27.95, 39.95 are being interpreted as Kč, not EUR/
     USD. That is why Markets converts them into tiny USD/EUR amounts.
  4. Markets are active and converting. Czechia, European Union, and International markets are active. Shopify Markets is using local
     currencies. That layer appears to be working; it is converting from bad base values.
  5. Changing base currency appears possible in principle, but not trivial. Shopify’s current docs say store owners can change store currency
     in General settings, but they also warn that product price amounts do not convert, shipping rates must be rebuilt, apps/payment
     compatibility must be reviewed, reports/refunds/pending payments can be affected, and PayPal compatibility must be checked. I did not
     see a clean one-click “change to EUR” path exposed safely enough to use during this run.

  Sources used: Shopify Help Center on changing store currency
  (https://help.shopify.com/en/manual/payments/shopify-payments/store-currency/changing-your-store-currency), store vs payout currency
  (https://help.shopify.com/en/manual/payments/shopify-payments/store-currency/understanding-store-and-payout-currencies), and local currency
  pricing (https://help.shopify.com/en/manual/international/pricing).

  Recommendation
  Use the simpler operational path: keep CZK as the store base currency and build the international store around Shopify Markets.

  Reason: the store is Czech-located, Shopify Payments is already active, payout is tied to the local/EUR banking setup, Markets already
  supports EUR/USD display, and changing base currency would still require repricing, shipping-rate review, payment review, and tax review.

Willardmechem: • Cause

  The restyled tab bar in development/microcontractor-mvp/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx:31 is position: "absolute" with height: 86 and a bottom offset. That makes
  it visually float, but React Navigation no longer reserves vertical layout space for it. The shared scroll padding was still only spacing.xl + spacing.lg =
  56, which is less than the tab bar’s 86 + 18 footprint, so bottom content could scroll underneath it.

  Changed Files

  - development/microcontractor-mvp/src/lib/layout.ts:1: added shared floating tab bar dimensions and clearance helpers.
  - development/microcontractor-mvp/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx:4: tab bar now uses the shared constants and includes safe-area-aware bottom offset.
  - development/microcontractor-mvp/src/components/Screen.tsx:11: shared scroll screens now get bottom padding equal to the floating tab bar clearance.
  - development/microcontractor-mvp/src/features/estimates/EstimateForm.tsx:135: estimate form gets the same clearance because it uses its own ScrollView.

  Why This Is Structurally Correct

  The tab bar visual metrics and content clearance now come from one shared source. If the floating tab height or bottom offset changes again, the content
  padding changes with it. This preserves the stronger tab bar styling instead of reverting it, and fixes the shared scroll pattern rather than patching
  individual tab screens.

  Verification

  3. Exact ranking/snippet heuristic used

  Ranking heuristic (deterministic per row):

  - phrase_bonus = 100 if lowercase full query appears as exact substring in lowercase memory_text, else 0
  - coverage = number of distinct query terms present
  - frequency = total count of all query terms in text
  - score = phrase_bonus + (coverage * 10) + frequency
  - sort by (score, indexed_at) descending

  Snippet heuristic:

  - normalize whitespace to a compact one-line string
  - target max length 120 chars
  - if text already short, return as-is
  - otherwise find earliest query-term occurrence
  - return a window around that hit with ... prefix/suffix when clipped
  - fallback to front-truncated preview if no term location found

  2. What producer path I implemented

  - Added a real shared handler path: produce_open_loops(paths, payload) in service/handlers.py.
  - Added CLI invocation: agent-diary produce-open-loops [--limit N] [--entry-ids ...].
  - Producer flow:
      - selects bounded source entries (recent window via limit or explicit entry_ids)
      - analyzes raw entry content with simple loop heuristics
      - builds analysis:open-loop artifact content JSON per design-note shape
      - attaches artifact through existing attach_artifact path (so storage behavior stays consistent)
  - Result is stored as an artifact JSON file; raw entries are unchanged.

  3. Exact first-pass heuristic used

  - Candidate detection:
      - split content into line/sentence-ish chunks
      - keep chunks that include unresolved hints:
          - todo, follow up, follow-up, pending, unresolved, need to, should, or ?
      - drop chunks that include closure hints:
          - done, completed, resolved, closed, fixed, cancelled/canceled
  - Loop grouping:
      - normalize candidate text to a lowercase alnum key and group similar mentions
  - loop_id:
      - deterministic SHA1 prefix of normalized_text + supporting_entry_ids
  - Strength/confidence:
      - mentions >= 3 → high, 0.85
      - mentions == 2 → medium, 0.70
      - mentions == 1 → low, 0.55
  - Lineage:
      - supporting_entry_ids per loop
      - artifact metadata includes source_entry_ids, analysis_window, schema_version, method, method_version, generated_at
  - Anchor:
      - artifact entry_id set to newest source entry (as defined in design note).
